requirements.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list
Notes are displayed with their titles visible
User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes
User can access a search function
Search input field is visible or accessible
User can access an AI chat or assistant feature
Chat interface has an input field for questions
User can see a button or option to create a new note
New note creation is accessible from the main screen
Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy
Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes
Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note
Formatting toolbar or options are available
User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)
App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)
Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile
Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a hierarchical list of notes visible in the app's interface. The notes are organized into categories with subcategories, allowing users to navigate through them and view each note clearly. This satisfies the requirement of 'User can view all notes in a hierarchical list'.
results.notes: The design of the notes section appears to be well-organized and user-friendly, indicating a thoughtful implementation of the hierarchical list functionality.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

results.evidence: In the image provided, we can observe a note-listing interface where several notes are displayed, and their titles are visible.
results.notes: The app appears to meet the requirement of displaying notes with their titles visible. The user interface is designed in a way that allows for easy access and understanding of the different notes, their priorities, and their details.

requirement.description: User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a sidebar navigation option visible at the bottom of the screen. This meets the requirement 'User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes'. The sidebar contains various categories such as 'My Notes', 'Unread', 'Favorite', and 'Trash', which suggests that users can use this navigation bar to view their notes.
results.notes: The text 'View Notes' is visible next to the sidebar navigation, indicating that it is a link to open or close the sidebar. This also supports the functionality of browsing notes. Additionally, there are several news articles listed on the screen, which may be related to the notes functionality.

requirement.description: User can access a search function

results.notes: The search function is visibly available with a search icon and placeholder text. It appears to be the standard functionality for iOS apps.

requirement.description: Search input field is visible or accessible

results.evidence: In the screenshot provided, there is a search input field visible at the top of the screen with a magnifying glass icon next to it, indicating that searching is accessible. The search bar has a placeholder text 'Search Jobs, Recruiters or Companies' and seems to be functional.

requirement.description: User can access an AI chat or assistant feature

results.evidence: The image displays a screenshot of an iOS app interface where there is an AI chat or assistant feature accessible through the search bar at the top. A user can start a conversation by typing into the 'Search Journals' text field and pressing the magnifying glass icon on the keyboard.
results.notes: While it is not possible to test the functionality of the AI chat from this screenshot, the design and layout suggest that it is an integrated part of the app and accessible for users.

requirement.description: Chat interface has an input field for questions

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is an input field for questions at the bottom of the chat interface which allows users to type and submit their inquiries or messages. This meets the requirement.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: The image shows a screenshot of a mobile application interface with a navigation menu on the left side displaying various categories such as 'Class Reflections', 'News Items', and other options. There is no explicit button or option labeled 'Create Note' in this view of the app.
results.notes: It is not clear if there are additional screens or features within the application that may include a create note function, or if it exists in a different section of the app. The design suggests that creating notes may be an additional step, so further investigation would be needed to confirm this requirement is met.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a note creation button on the main screen of the app.
results.notes: The requirement 'New note creation is accessible from the main screen' has been met. The note creation button is clearly visible and easily accessible from the main screen.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a messaging app with a chat where multiple messages are grouped together under a common thread. The grouping of messages indicates support for nesting or hierarchy within the 'Notes' category.
results.notes: While it is clear that messages can be organized by grouping them, I cannot ascertain if notes have specific nesting levels beyond two (the two visible groups).

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a clear indentation or folder structure for nested notes within an iOS app, indicating that this requirement has been met.
results.notes: There are several levels of indentation, with each level representing a subfolder or subcategory. This helps users quickly identify the hierarchy and context of their notes.

requirement.description: Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note

results.evidence: No editor area is visible in this screenshot for viewing or editing a note.
results.notes: It is unclear from this image whether the 'Editor' area would be accessible from the settings menu or not.

requirement.description: Formatting toolbar or options are available

results.evidence: A search bar is visible on top of the screen, but no formatting toolbar or options are immediately evident in this view of the app. Additionally, there is a button labeled 'class reflections,' which suggests that users can access a page dedicated to class reflections, but it's unclear if these pages provide additional formatting options.
results.notes: It's possible that the formatting toolbar or options might be available in other parts of the app not visible in this screenshot. The search bar and 'class reflections' button are the only UI elements related to formatting.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

results.evidence: The screenshot displays a user interface for an app on an iOS device. The main content area shows block-level content, including paragraphs and headings, with clear typography and spacing that allow the user to distinguish between different levels of hierarchy. There is also a list visible in this section, which further supports the availability of structured content within the app.
results.notes: The only limitation noted in the screenshot is that it is not possible to determine if there are any lists on other parts of the app as they are not visible in this particular view. However, based on what's shown in this screenshot, the user can clearly see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, and lists) within the editor section.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile application interface with a bottom tab navigation design. This is considered mobile-appropriate as it follows the standard UI design pattern for touch-based devices where users tap on individual tabs to navigate through different sections of the app.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: In the screenshot, all touch targets in the UI, such as buttons and navigation links, are of an appropriate size for a mobile device based on standard design guidelines. There is enough space between elements to accommodate comfortable finger taps.
results.notes: It's essential to consider that different devices have different screen sizes and resolutions, so it's crucial to ensure touch targets are accessible and easy to tap on various devices. The app in this image seems to be responsive and well-designed for mobile use.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.notes: The content seems to be horizontally scrollable as there is a visible scroll indicator on the right side of the screen.
